nodes.context: Faction of the Genovese crime family operating in East Harlem and the Bronx.
One of the Five Families of New York, parent organization of the 116th Street Crew.
Italian-American murder for hire and drug trafficking gang operating in 1970s Italian Harlem.
Early Sicilian Mafia family in New York, founders of the 116th Street Crew.
Neapolitan crime organization, involved in conflict with Morello family in the Mafia–Camorra War.
One of the Five Families, listed as an ally of the 116th Street Crew.

edges.evidence_quote: The 116th Street Crew , also known as the Uptown Crew , is a faction of the Genovese crime family .
edges.context: The 116th Street Crew is a faction (sub-unit) of the Genovese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1970s and early 1980s, the 116th Street Crew had absorbed and initiated many former members of the vicious East Harlem Purple Gang
edges.context: The 116th Street Crew absorbed former members of the East Harlem Purple Gang.
The Morello crime family founded the 116th Street Crew.

edges.evidence_quote: Gaetano "Tommaso/Thomas" Lo Monte took control of the policy game in Harlem but murdered on October 13, 1915. Nicholo Morello took over and became embroiled in the Mafia-Camorra War . This conflict was between the Sicilian Morello-Terranova family and Brooklyn Camorra gangs led by Pellegrino Morano .
edges.context: The Morello family fought the Camorra in the Mafia–Camorra War.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino , Lucchese , Purple Gang
edges.context: The Bonanno crime family is listed as an ally of the 116th Street Crew.
